title: The Burden of Loyalty
notes: Collection/anthology row present; individual contents not explicitly recoverable from TLBranson list.

curation.synopsis: Eight tales from the long fault-line of the Heresy. Chris Wraight's novella The Wolf King watches Leman Russ argue the price of obedience after the burning of Prospero. Rob Sanders's Cybernetica follows a Mechanicum thinking engine that learns the war is not the war it was built for. Around them, shorter stories pull at the edges of canon — Dan Abnett's Perpetual on a deathless agent of Malcador, David Annandale's The Binary Succession on the politics of a fracturing Mars, Aaron Dembski-Bowden's Into Exile on a Knight Errant cutting away from the rest of his order, John French's Ordo Sinister on a Titan crew already too far gone, L J Goulding's The Heart of the Pharos on what was buried beneath Sotha, and Gav Thorpe's The Thirteenth Wolf on a vendetta the Vlka Fenryka would rather forget. Loyalty here is never simple, and never cheap.

flags.reason: anthology-wide cast is broad; only stories with clearly named lead POVs surfaced in available sources.
